Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-20T23:17:37Z","updated_at":"2024-09-20T23:17:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

cross() fails with 1D arrays, int numbers, with dtype=object. Using dtype=object since intermediate calculations with actual numbers will overflow int64.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
u = np.array([1, 2, -1], dtype=object)
v = np.array([-1, 1, 0], dtype=object)
np.cross(u, v)
```


### Error message:

```shell
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
    np.cross(u, v)
  File "C:\Users\grant\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python311\Lib\site-packages\numpy\_core\numeric.py", line 1731, in cross
    multiply(a0, b2, out=tmp)
numpy._core._exceptions._UFuncOutputCastingError: Cannot cast ufunc 'multiply' output from dtype('O') to dtype('int64') with casting rule 'same_kind'
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

3.11.1
2.1.0

### Runtime Environment:

_No response_

### Context for the issue:

Worked around by changing shape to (1, 3), taking cross-product, then changing shape back to (3). Also works if with Decimal numbers.

See also https://stackoverflow.com/questions/78988320/numpy-error-in-cross-with-dtype-object-arrays?noredirect=1#comment139277481_78988320","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27435/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27435/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
